ucs.title: [LLM] FIN13 / Elephant Beetle: Java app-server (WebSphere/WebLogic/JBoss/Tomcat) or IIS spawning OS recon/admin binaries — web-shell command execu
ucs.description: FIN13 (Mandiant cluster, aka Sygnia 'Elephant Beetle') achieves initial access by exploiting public-facing Java/IIS app servers and dropping JSP/.NET web shells, then issues OS commands through them. The most reliable host-side signal is a Java or IIS worker process spawning cmd/PowerShell or recon binaries — a process tree that is virtually never legitimate on a properly maintained app server. This UC targets the recon-from-web-shell stage that immediately precedes their long-dwell LATAM financial-fraud operations.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_path) as child_paths values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("java.exe","javaw.exe","w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","PresentationHost.exe","jboss.exe","jrun.exe")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","wmic.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe")) AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// FIN13 / Elephant Beetle — web-shell execution: Java or IIS worker spawning OS / recon binaries
let _appserver_parents = dynamic(["java.exe","javaw.exe","w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","jboss.exe","jrun.exe"]);
let _recon_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","wmic.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_appserver_parents)
| where FileName in~ (_recon_children)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                       // skip machine accounts
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentProc  = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildProc   = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// FIN13 / Elephant Beetle — web-shell execution chain (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let _appserver_parents = dynamic(["java.exe","javaw.exe","w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","jboss.exe","jrun.exe"]);
let _recon_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","wmic.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                  // process create
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tostring(split(ParentProcessName, '\\')[-1])
| extend ChildLeaf  = tostring(split(NewProcessName, '\\')[-1])
| where ParentLeaf in~ (_appserver_parents)
| where ChildLeaf  in~ (_recon_children)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: FIN13 Web Shell Recon Execution via Java or IIS Worker
id: 7c8b3e0a-fe21-4d8a-9b59-fin13-webshell-recon
status: experimental
description: Detects Java application-server (Tomcat/WebSphere/WebLogic/JBoss) or IIS w3wp.exe processes spawning OS shells or recon binaries — characteristic of FIN13 (Elephant Beetle) JSP/ASPX web-shell command execution.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/fin13-cybercriminal-mexico
  - https://www.sygnia.co/threat-reports/elephant-beetle-uncovering-an-organized-financial-theft-operation/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.g1016
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_process:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\jboss.exe'
  child_process:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  condition: parent_process and child_process
falsepositives:
  - Application deployment scripts that legitimately shell out (rare; should be inventoried)
  - Monitoring agents installed inside the JVM/IIS context
level: high
ucs.rationale: FIN13's hallmark per Mandiant and Sygnia is multi-year dwell on internet-facing Java application servers in LATAM finance/retail/hospitality, achieved via JSP/.NET web shells dropped after exploiting app-server vulns (T1190 → T1505.003). On a healthy enterprise app server, java.exe/javaw.exe/w3wp.exe should never spawn cmd.exe, powershell.exe or recon LOLBins like nltest, whoami, net or wmic — whe
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN13 long-dwell SSH reverse-tunnel / port-forwarding via plink, ssh.exe or PuTTY (T1572)
ucs.description: Sygnia documented Elephant Beetle / FIN13 maintaining persistence inside victim networks for months-to-years by using legitimate SSH clients (plink, OpenSSH ssh.exe, PuTTY) to build reverse tunnels and port-forwards from internal app servers back to attacker infrastructure, smuggling C2 and lateral-movement traffic over TCP/22. This UC catches the SSH-tunnel command-line shape they characteristically use.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("plink.exe","ssh.exe","putty.exe","pscp.exe") AND (Processes.process="* -R *" OR Processes.process="* -L *" OR Processes.process="* -D *" OR Processes.process="* -N *" OR Processes.process="*StrictHostKeyChecking=no*" OR Processes.process="*0.0.0.0:*" OR Processes.process="*127.0.0.1:*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdlines "(?i)-(?<tunnel_flag>[RLDN])\s+(?<forward_spec>\S+)" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// FIN13 / Elephant Beetle — SSH reverse-tunnel / port-forward for long-dwell C2
let _ssh_clients = dynamic(["plink.exe","ssh.exe","putty.exe","pscp.exe","openssh.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ (_ssh_clients)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(^|\s)-[RLDN](\s|$)"
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("StrictHostKeyChecking=no","0.0.0.0:","127.0.0.1:","-pw "," -i ","-fNT","-fNR","-fNL")
| extend TunnelFlag = extract(@"(?i)(?:^|\s)-([RLDN])(?:\s|$)", 1, ProcessCommandLine),
         ForwardSpec = extract(@"(?i)(?:^|\s)-[RLD]\s+(\S+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
// pivot: which app-server / DMZ host launched it?
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceInfo
    | summarize arg_max(Timestamp, OSPlatform, IsInternetFacing, MachineGroup) by DeviceName
  ) on DeviceName
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, MachineGroup, IsInternetFacing,
          AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          TunnelFlag, ForwardSpec,
          ParentProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// FIN13 / Elephant Beetle — SSH tunneling (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let _ssh_clients = dynamic(["plink.exe","ssh.exe","putty.exe","pscp.exe","openssh.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, '\\')[-1]))
| where ChildLeaf in~ (_ssh_clients)
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(^|\s)-[RLDN](\s|$)"
   or CommandLine has_any ("StrictHostKeyChecking=no","0.0.0.0:","127.0.0.1:","-pw "," -i ","-fNT","-fNR","-fNL")
| extend TunnelFlag = extract(@"(?i)(?:^|\s)-([RLDN])(?:\s|$)", 1, CommandLine),
         ForwardSpec = extract(@"(?i)(?:^|\s)-[RLD]\s+(\S+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TunnelFlag, ForwardSpec
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: FIN13 SSH Reverse-Tunnel / Port-Forward Command Line
id: 5b9d12a4-cc6f-4a44-a2c1-fin13-ssh-tunnel
status: experimental
description: Detects use of plink, ssh.exe or PuTTY with reverse-tunnel / dynamic-proxy / local-forward switches — FIN13 (Elephant Beetle) tradecraft for multi-year persistence in LATAM finance environments.
references:
  - https://www.sygnia.co/threat-reports/elephant-beetle-uncovering-an-organized-financial-theft-operation/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/fin13-cybercriminal-mexico
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.t1090.001
  - attack.g1016
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  ssh_client:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\plink.exe'
      - '\ssh.exe'
      - '\putty.exe'
      - '\pscp.exe'
      - '\openssh.exe'
  tunnel_flags:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)(^|\s)-[RLDN](\s|$)'
  bypass_strings:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'StrictHostKeyChecking=no'
      - '0.0.0.0:'
      - '127.0.0.1:'
      - '-fNT'
      - '-fNR'
      - '-fNL'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: ssh_client and (tunnel_flags or bypass_strings) and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Authorised devops / SRE jump-host workflows (allowlist by host group)
  - Git over SSH from build hosts (typically no -R/-L/-D flag, will not match)
level: high
ucs.rationale: FIN13's distinguishing operational pattern (per Sygnia's Elephant Beetle report) is patient, low-and-slow access via legitimate SSH clients used as reverse tunnels — not malware C2. They favour plink and OpenSSH because the binaries are signed and the traffic blends with admin tooling. Detecting -R/-L/-D/-N flag patterns on Windows servers in the financial/retail enterprise (where SSH outbound is
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
